The correct interpretation is option A. The premise "Wn ∨ Wm" means "Either Nancy or Marvin are at work." The conditional "(∀x)[Lx ⊃ (Dx ⊃ ~Wx)]" translates to "All lawyers are not at work if they are out to dinner." The conclusion "~(∀x)~Wx" means "Not everything is not at work," which matches option A.
